case_memory.chief_complaint: ___ yo woman with past medical history of endometrial
current_turn_information: I’ve been constipated for 12 days, but lately I’ve had several days of diarrhea that looks like mucus.
I’m also having cramping pain in my lower abdomen, which started about a month ago and gets worse when I strain to have a bowel movement — even after taking Miralax and Mag Citrate.
When I strained recently, I got lightheaded, then dizzy, and lost consciousness.
I was on the floor for about an hour before my husband found me.
I felt like my ankle “broke” when I came to.
I’m currently breathing fine on room air, but I still have abdominal discomfort.
I don’t have fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, or any blood in my stool or urine.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old female, admitted via ER for EU OBSERVATION. History: endometrial adenocarcinoma s/p TAH + BSO, hypothyroidism, hypercholesterolemia, allergic rhinitis, tonsillectomy. Social: family history of asthma, depression (mother), heart murmur (sister). Presenting with constipation x12 days, mucus-like diarrhea, cramping lower abdominal pain x1 month, lightheadedness/dizziness/loss of consciousness with straining, right ankle fracture (splinted), abdominal discomfort. Vitals: T 99.1, BP 118/68, HR 61, RR 18, O2 sat 96% RA. Physical: soft abdomen, no guarding/rebound, right basilar rales, splint on right ankle, diminished RLE strength. Denies fever, chills, night sweats, hematochezia, melena, hematuria, dysuria. Final result: Fracture of lateral malleolus, closed. Knowledge base: Basophilia definition, causes, diagnosis.

query.query_text: abdominal pain with constipation and mucus diarrhea; new report of syncope with straining and ankle fracture; need memory about targeted neuro/cardiac history before imaging; avoid premature diagnosis
